Off Calendar Jan-18-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Jeffrey Workman, 415.722.1157 for Cross Defendant/Moving party Brian Rangel.
Glen Broemer, 805.663.2654 for Defendant/Opposing party Sally Liu.

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: CROSS DEFENDANT BRIAN RANGEL MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Request for Judicial notice is granted. No opposition filed to the request for judicial notice and Cross-Complainant relies on the contents of the settlement agreement it is opposition. Motion is GRANTED without leave to amend as to the 13th and 14th causes of action. Cross-Complainant concedes that these causes of action are barred as to the sole moving cross-defendant. Motion to Strike references to Rangel from the 4th cause of action is GRANTED with leave to amend. Cross-Complainant concedes that cause of action for ejectment "is appropriate where the landlord seeks to recover possession from a tenant who has breached the lease." (Opposition 2: 19-20). However, no facts are alleged as to Ragel breaching any lease he is a party to. Motion is GRANTED with leave to amend to allege facts (not conclusions) supporting malice, oppression or fraud by Rangel. Motion is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts giving rise to claim for attorney's fees, e.g. statute or binding contract. Cross-Complainant's counsel is ordered to comply with CRC 2.104 and 2.105 in future.
Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
